title: LangGraph Architecture
learning_outcomes: Explain stateful execution graphs and node function definitions.
Formulate state reducer operations and consolidation logic.

content_sections.title: Stateful Execution Graphs
content_sections.content: While simple chains execute sequentially, complex workflows require state tracking and cyclic execution paths. **LangGraph** models workflows as stateful directed graphs, where nodes represent actions (e.g. calling an LLM or a tool) and edges define execution flow. The graph shares a global state object passed between nodes during execution.

content_sections.title: State Reducers and Consolidation
content_sections.content:
To prevent concurrent nodes from overwriting the global state, LangGraph uses **reducers**. A reducer defines how node updates are merged into the state (e.g. appending new messages to a list rather than overwriting it):

$$\mathbf{S}_{t+1} = \text{Reducer}(\mathbf{S}_t, \Delta \mathbf{S})$$

This guarantees state consistency across parallel execution branches.
